name: Protagonist Therapeutics, Inc
business: Protagonist Therapeutics, Inc. is an integrated discovery and development company focused on developing peptide-based therapeutics for inflammatory, hematologic, and metabolic diseases. Its lead product candidates include icotyde (for psoriasis) licensed to JNJ and rusfertide (for polycythemia vera) partnered with Takeda. The company also has earlier-stage oral peptide programs targeting IL-17, obesity, and oral hepcidin.
sellsTo: Patients with psoriasis, polycythemia vera, immune-mediated skin diseases, and obesity

exposures.sentence: Icotyde (icotrokinra) is a first-in-class investigational targeted oral peptide that selectively blocks the Interleukin-23 receptor (IL-23R), which underpins the inflammatory response in psoriasis and offers potential in other IL-23-mediated diseases.
Rusfertide is a first-in-class investigational injectable mimetic of the natural hormone hepcidin in development for the treatment of the rare blood disorder polycythemia vera (PV).
We are developing PN-881, a potential best-in-class oral peptide IL-17 antagonist, for the treatment of immune-mediated skin diseases.

In June 2025, we nominated PN-477, a potential best-in-class novel triple GLP-1, GIP and GCG receptor agonist peptide with oral (PN-477o) and subcutaneous (PN-477sc) routes of administration, as a development candidate for the treatment of obesity.
In December 2025, we nominated development candidate PN-458, a potential best-in-class novel dual GLP-1 and GIP receptor agonist peptide, as a development candidate for the treatment of obesity.

In December 2025, we nominated development candidate PN-8047, an orally administered hepcidin functional mimetic small molecule, which we believe may be complementary to the injectable rusfertide for offering the best treatment options for PV.

Our proprietary technology platform is purposefully built to exploit the advantages of constrained peptides, which are much smaller than antibody-based drugs and may be delivered orally but are big enough to bind and block the difficult targets that antibodies bind and modulate.

catalysts.event: JNJ submitted NDA to FDA for icotyde in plaque psoriasis
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025 and September 2025, respectively, JNJ submitted a New Drug Application (NDA) to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and an application to the European Medicines Agency (EMA) seeking the first approval of Icotyde for the treatment of adults and pediatric patients 12 years of age and older with moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis.

catalysts.event: Takeda submitted NDA to FDA for rusfertide in PV
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, Takeda submitted an NDA to the FDA for rusfertide in PV.
Rusfertide has also received Orphan Drug status, Fast Track designation and, in August 2025, Breakthrough Therapy designation (BTD).

catalysts.event: First human dosed in PN-881 Phase 1 trial
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, the first human subject was dosed in our Phase 1 trial of PN-881 (ClinicalTrials.gov identifier NCT07153146) evaluating its safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics in healthy adults.

catalysts.event: Expected launch of icotyde in the United States
catalysts.sentence: JNJ has disclosed that it expects to launch Icotyde in the United States in 2026, subject to regulatory approval.

catalysts.event: Expected launch of rusfertide in second half of 2026
catalysts.sentence: Takeda has disclosed that it expects to launch rusfertide in the second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approval.

catalysts.event: Expected completion of PN-881 Phase 1 study in mid-2026 and initiation of Phase 2 by end of 2026
catalysts.sentence: We expect to complete the Phase 1 study in mid-2026 and initiate a Phase 2 study of PN-881 in psoriasis by the end of 2026.

catalysts.event: Anticipated initiation of Phase 1 clinical studies for PN-477sc (mid-2026) and PN-477o (second half 2026)
catalysts.sentence: IND-enabling studies of PN-477 are underway and the initiation of Phase 1 clinical studies in PN-477sc and PN-477o are anticipated by mid-2026 and in the second half of 2026, respectively.

catalysts.event: Expected exercise of opt-out right from Takeda U.S. profit/loss sharing in Q2 2026
catalysts.sentence: We currently expect to exercise that right in the second quarter of 2026, within the 90-day opt-out window beginning 120 days after the NDA filing date as prescribed in the agreement.

tamClaims.claim: Psoriasis affects approximately 130 million people worldwide and over 8 million people in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: Psoriasis is a chronic inflammatory skin disease affecting approximately 130 million people worldwide and over 8 million people in the United States, or 2% to 3% of the adult population.

tamClaims.claim: Global sales for psoriasis therapies were $25.4 billion in 2024 and are expected to exceed $39.0 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: Global sales for psoriasis therapies were $25.4 billion in 2024 and are expected to exceed $39.0 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Global market sales of PsA therapies were approximately $7.1 billion in 2024 and are expected to reach more than $10.0 billion in 2034.
tamClaims.sentence: Global market sales of PsA therapies were approximately $7.1 billion in 2024 and are expected to reach more than $10.0 billion in 2034.

tamClaims.claim: Global HS therapy sales were approximately $1.5 billion in 2024, with global sales projected to reach $6.5 billion by 2034.
tamClaims.sentence: Global HS therapy sales were approximately $1.5 billion in 2024, with global sales projected to reach $6.5 billion ($5.4 billion in the United States) by 2034.

tamClaims.claim: Global axSpA therapy sales were $4.7 billion in 2024, with global sales projected to reach $7.2 billion by 2034.
tamClaims.sentence: Global axSpA therapy sales were $4.7 billion in 2024, with global sales projected to reach $7.2 billion ($5.9 billion in the United States) by 2034.

tamClaims.claim: Global 2023 sales for UC therapies were $7.8 billion, projected to reach $18.8 billion by 2030; for CD therapies were $15.2 billion, projected to reach $18.7 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: Global 2023 sales for UC therapies were $7.8 billion, with global sales projected to reach $18.8 billion by 2030. Global 2023 sales for CD therapies were $15.2 billion, with global sales projected to reach $18.7 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 155,000 patients diagnosed with PV in the United States, with about 78,000 treated.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 155,000 patients diagnosed with PV in the United States, of whom approximately 78,000 are treated, with a similar number of diagnosed patients in Europe.

tamClaims.claim: Over one billion adults globally live with obesity; in the US, nearly 40% of adults are obese and nearly 9% severely obese.
tamClaims.sentence: Globally, over one billion adults are estimated to live with obesity. In the United States, nearly 40% of adults meet criteria for obesity and nearly 9% meet criteria for severe obesity.

tamClaims.claim: The global market for anti-obesity medications is expected to reach more than $120 billion by 2034.
tamClaims.sentence: The global market for anti-obesity medications is expected to reach more than $120 billion by 2034.

tamClaims.claim: JNJ projects peak year sales for icotyde at greater than $5.0 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: At JNJ's Enterprise Business Review in December 2023, JNJ highlighted Icotyde as a potential first- and best-in-class drug with potential across multiple indications, including plaque psoriasis, PsA and inflammatory bowel disease, with potential peak year sales projected at greater than $5.0 billion.

tamClaims.claim: Rusfertide has a peak revenue potential of $1.0 to $2.0 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: Rusfertide provides consistent hematocrit control and can potentially reduce treatment burden to achieve a peak revenue potential of $1.0 to $2.0 billion.
